paper_title: Fresh Tomatoes: Ideas to Build a Productive Eco-System
research_answer: Albania receives the lowest average price for fresh tomatoes across Europe and Asia, earning between $0.45 and $0.72 per kg — less than 60% of what higher-end markets command. To put this in context, average import prices in the broader European/Eurasian region range from $1.29/kg in Russia to $2.21/kg in Switzerland. So Albanian producers are being paid roughly a third of what the top-end markets pay. This extreme price discount reflects a combination of factors: Albania's exports go almost exclusively to lower-value neighboring markets (Kosovo, Serbia, Montenegro, Macedonia, Bulgaria, Greece), its production is highly atomized with no quality control or traceability, and its collectors operate on a spot-transaction basis with little market knowledge. In short, Albania is participating in the tomato trade but capturing very little of its value.
supporting_quotes: the prices that Albanian producers get is very low - the lowest in Europe for some products like tomatoes
Albanian tomatoes are paid less than 60% of the price received by higher-end markets. It ranges from about $ 0.45/Kg to $/Kg 0.72 in the best case
the average import prices (which vary a lot throughout the year) range from a minimum of $/Kg 1.29 in Russia to a maximum of $/Kg 2.21 in Switzerland
Albania receives the lowest average price for its fresh tomatoes in Europe and Asia
